title: Brown Sugar–Mustard Glazed Ham
description: A holiday-worthy baked ham with a caramelized brown sugar, whole grain mustard, and honey glaze. Cooked low and slow under foil to stay moist, then finished at high heat for a crisp, flavorful crust.

ingredients.name: 10-lb smoked bone-in ham with rind, preferably shank end
unsweetened apple juice or apple cider

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 325°F. Place ham in a large roasting pan and pour apple juice over. Cover ham completely with parchment paper, then cover pan tightly with heavy-duty foil. Bake until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part reads 145°F, about 3.5–4 hours. For a 10-lb ham, start checking temperature at the 3-hour mark. Remove from oven and increase temperature to 375°F.

Remove foil and parchment and discard the liquid from the pan. Trim the rind and all but a ¼-inch layer of fat. Score the fat in a crosshatch pattern about 1 inch apart, being careful not to cut into the flesh.

Spread mustard over the scored fat, then press brown sugar firmly over the mustard. Drizzle honey over the top. Return to oven and bake, basting occasionally with any glaze that drips into the pan, until well glazed, 30–35 minutes.
Transfer ham to a platter and rest at least 45 minutes before carving. Serve warm or at room temperature.

notes:
Use 20 minutes per pound as a cook time guide rather than the recipe's fixed time — it scales better for smaller hams. A 6.5-lb ham works beautifully at this rate.

Unsweetened cranberry juice is a good substitute for apple juice and adds a pleasant tartness.

After removing the ham from the initial bake, the drained juices can be used as a base for a tangy pan gravy — worth doing rather than discarding.

The shank end is easier to carve than the butt end. Buy with the rind on; it protects against dryness during the long cook.
